provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This scheme may be cited as the Fire and Rescue Services Scheme 2007 and shall come into operation on the publication.

provisions.title: Applica­tion
provisions.content: This scheme shall apply to all Fire and Rescue Service personnel.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this scheme , unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Public Services Act, [No. 8 of 2002] “ ad-hoc Committee ” means Committee established under regulation 9(3) of the Public Services Regulation to facilitate appointment of Public Servants in the Fire and Rescue Services ; “ Appellate Authority ” means a body or organ or a person Authorized by this scheme to decide upon Fire and Rescue Services personnel appeals; “ appointment ” means recruitment of persons in the Fire and Rescue Services and shall include the first appointment or appointments on promotion; “ Appointing Authority ” means a body or organ or person empowered by the Act to make appointments of the Fire and Rescue Services personnel in the Services; “ approved establishment ” means the establishment of posts in the Fire and Rescue Services of which there is provision in the approved budget of their establishment; “ Brigade ” means organized unit of the Fire and Rescue Services within a specific area or in relation to a particular public authority; and the term when used in relation to any area, authority, corporation, region means the brigade for that area, authority corporation or Region; “ Calamity ” means an occurrence by which life and property is endangered; “ Commissioner ” means the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services ; “ constitution ” means the constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania, 1977; “ Criminal Offence ” means an offence chargeable in the criminal Courts as defined under criminal procedures Act , 1985; “ Directorate ” means an identified entity within the organizational structure for management; “ disciplinary authority ” means any person or authority vested with power under Part IV of the Public Services Regulations Act of 2003 Part IV of this scheme ‘to take disciplinary measures against any Fire and Rescue Services personnel and includes any person to whom those powers have been delegated; “ District Fire Officer ” Means a senior Fire Officer appointed to be in charge of the district; “ employee ” means any person employed in the Fire and Rescue services ; “ employer ” Means a person or organization in the Fire and Rescue services , which Fire and Rescue employee entered into a contract of service and who is responsible for the payment of salaries of such Fire and Rescue employee ; “ Fire and Rescue Services ”, means the Fire and Rescue Services; “ Fire Authority ” means the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services -and includes officer authorized by him generally or specifically to exercise powers, functions and duties conferred by the scheme ; “ Fireman ” means any member of the Fire and Rescue Services including those from Urban Fire Brigade , Port Fire Brigade and Airport Fire Brigade ; “ Fire Officer ” means any fireman of or above the rank of inspectors; “ Fire Station ” means any place appointed by the Commissioner to be a Fire Station. “ Fire hazard ” means:— (a) Any alteration to any building in contravention of the regulations on fire precautions in building, petrol stations and sports ground; (b) The overcrowding of any place of public entertainment or public assembly such as might render escape in the event of fire or other calamity materially more difficult; (c) Any removal from any building of any fire Services installation or equipments which was provided in such building in accordance with plans certified by the Commissioner for the purpose of the provision of the Building Rules; (d) Any other matter or circumstances which materially increases the likelihood of fire or other calamity or the danger of life or property that would result from the outbreak of fire or the occurrence of any other calamity or which would materially hamper the Services in the discharge of its duties in the event of fire or other calamity; “ Gazetted Officer ” means a Fire Officer of or above the rank of Assistant Superintendent ; “ Inspector ” include an Assistant Inspector; “ Intoxicating liquor ” has the meaning assigned to it in the intoxication liquor Act ; “ Junior Fire Officer ” means Fire Officer of or below the rank of Inspector ; “leave” means an authorized absence from duty and include annual leave, maternity leave, paternity leave, sabbatical leave, sick leave, leave pending retirement, leave without pay and convalescent leave; “ Minister ” means the Minister for the time being responsible for Fire and Rescue Services ; “ Non Commissioned Officer ” means a Fire Officer of the rank of Sergeant Major, Station Sergeant, Sergeant, Corporal and Fireman ; “ Officer in Charge ” means a Fire Officer appointed by the Commissioner to be in charge of a Fire Station ; “ Pensionable Officer ” means a member of the Services whose Services is pensionable under Public Services Retirement Benefit Act amending or replacing the same for the time being in the Services; “ Permanent Secretary ” means the Permanent Secretary for the time being responsible for Fire and, Rescue Services; “ Premises ” includes any place or conveyance; “ Property ” including any movable property, money or valuable security; “ Public Services ” means the system or organization entrusted with the responsibility of overseeing the provision or directly providing the general public with what they need from the government as permissible by laws, and include the Services in the Civil Services; teachers Services, the local government Services the health Services, the Immigration and the- Fire and Rescue Services and the operational Services; “ rank and files ” means firemen of the rank below that of inspector; “ recruit ” means a person undergoing training for a post in the Services who is a member of the Services but not a Fire Officer ; “ Regional Fire Officer ” means a Senior- Fire Officer appointed to be in-charge of the region; “ scheme ” means the Fire and Rescue Services Scheme of 2004; “ Senior Fire Officer ” means Fire Officer of or above the rank of Assistant Superintendent ; “ Superintendent ” includes Assistant and Senior Superintendent; “ services ” means the Fire and Rescue Services . Part II – Administration of the service

provisions.title: Powers of the President
provisions.content: (1) The President shall be the appointing authority of the Commissioner . (2) The Commissioner in the Fire and Rescue Services vested with powers under the Act , shall exercise those powers on behalf of the President and shall act with obedience Abiding to the directive issued in that respect. (3) All executive functions of Fire and Rescue Services shall be performed by Fire Officers on behalf of the President.

provisions.title: Powers of the Minister
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall be the head and overseer in the implementation of this scheme , policies and regulations governing the Fire and Rescue Services . (2) The Minister shall ensure effective implementation of this scheme of Fire and Rescue Services staff within their Fire Stations, Regions and Districts. (3) The minister may make regulations providing for the administration and command of Fire Stations, Fire Brigade , Regions and Districts. (4) The Minister shall coordinate and monitor the performance of Fire Stations, Brigades and of the Fire and Rescue Services for compliance with the National policies, guidelines and standards. (5) In the Exercise of the powers vested in by this section in regard to the operational control of the Services, the Commissioner shall be responsible and subject to directions and guidance of the Minister .

provisions.title: Powers of the Chief Secretary
provisions.content: The Chief Secretary as the Head and Chief Executive of Public Services Chief Secretary shall be the disciplinary authority of the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services and may, in that capacity in relation to any public servant exercise all or any of the powers delegated to a disciplinary authority .

provisions.title: Ministry responsible for Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: The Ministry responsible for Fire and Rescue Services shall be the Central organization responsible for the management and administration through which the government exercise control of:— (i) Urban; (ii) Port; (iii) Airport; and (iv) Other Brigades which may be established, and ensure that it is in good shape to serve as efficient and effective machinery for the execution of operations.

provisions.title: Powers of Permanent Secretary responsible for matters relating to Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: The Permanent Secretary responsible for Fire and Rescue services shall:— (a) be the authority in respect of the appointments and confirmation of Fire and Rescue employees of the rank of Assistant Inspector to the rank of Senior assistant Commissioner ; (b) notify the Chief Secretary of Vacancies in the post of Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services which the President is the appointing authority; (c) formulate, promulgate, monitor, evaluate, review and interpret administrative and personnel policies for Fire and Rescue Services .

provisions.title: The Immigration Fire and Rescue Services Department of the Public Services Commission
provisions.content: The Immigration, Fire and Rescue Services Department of the Public Services Commission shall be responsible for all matters related to the Fire and Rescue Services and shall enable the Public Services Commission perform its functions provided for in section 10 of the act; (a) monitoring the implementation of decisions made and directives issued by the Public Services Commission in terms of appointments, staff development, ethics and disciplinary issues in the Fire and Rescue Services ; (b) facilitating merit based recruitment; (c) advising the Secretary to the Public Services Commission on good governance of Fire and Rescue Services ; (d) receiving, scrutinizing, preparing summaries of Firemen and Fire Officers’ appeals and submit to the Public Services Commission for its decision; (e) monitoring and evaluating performance within Fire and Rescue Services .

provisions.title: Notification of vacancies
provisions.content: The Permanent Secretary responsible for Fire and Rescue Services shall:— (a) ensure that all appointments in the Fire and Rescue Services are in accordance with the approved establishment ; (b) notify the Public Services Commission of vacancies in the post of the Commissioner /Deputy Commissioner and Senior Assistant Commissioner ; (c) issue letters of appointment of Assistant Inspector to Assistant Commissioner ; (d) coordinate and ensure proper upkeep of personnel information of Fire and Rescue Services staff including their performance appraisal reports of Commissioner and Deputy Commissioner ; (e) perform any other duties and functions related to Fire and Rescue Services assigned to him by the Minister and the Chief Secretary.

provisions.title: Office of the Public Service Manage­ment
provisions.content: (1) The Office of Public Services Management shall be responsible for control of levels of staffing in the Fire and Rescue Services . (2) Facilitate mobility of Fire and Rescue Services within the service. (3) Carrying periodical review of Firemen and Fire Officers rates of pay and allowances.

provisions.title: Duties of Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: (1) The duties of Fire and Rescue Services shall be to:— (a) prevent and protect from Fire and calamities; (b) save life and properties; (c) extinguish fire; (d) carry out ambulance Services; (e) handling of hazardous material; (f) conducting fire safety inspection and issue certificates to premises and conveyances; (g) perform such other duties as may be imposed on it by law or by any other relevant authority. (2) Functions of the Comm­issioner Subject to the provisions of the Act and without prejudice to the generality of sub-clause (1), the Commissioner shall have the following functions:— (a) formulate policies on Fire Safety standards and advice the government with the view of securing their implementation; (b) make the necessary provisions for fire fighting and prevention at:— (i) Airports Units (ii) Urban Units (iii) Ports Units (iv) Mine and Industrial Units (v) Forest Units and (vi) Community based Units, according to international standards, practices and recommendations; (c) carry out research and training in all fire fighting and related fields and ensure the coordination of international aspects of such research and; training (d) supervise, instruct and monitor all fire and rescue Services activities of all levels in Tanzania mainland.

provisions.title: The Fire and Rescue Services ranks
provisions.content: The Fire and Rescue Services shall consist of the following ranks as the Public Services Management may direct in the following order of seniority. (a) gazetted Officer; (i) Commissioner (ii) Deputy Commissioner (iii) Senior Assistant Commissioner (iv) Assistant Commissioner (v) Senior Superintendent (vi) Superintendent (vii) Assistant Superintendent (b) inspector; (i) Inspectors (ii) Assistant Inspectors (c) non Commissioned Officers (i) Sergeant Major (ii) Station Sergeant (iii) Sergeant (iv) Corporal (v) Fireman

provisions.title: The Fire and Rescue Services units
provisions.content: The Fire and Rescue Services shall consist of units of Brigades and Fire Stations as the Commissioner may from time to time direct and shall include the following— (a) urban units; (b) airport units; (c) port units; (d) mines and Industrial units; (e) forest unit; (f) community based unit (Volunteer Fire Brigades).

provisions.title: The Fire and Rescue Services directo­rates
provisions.content: (1) The Fire and Rescue Services shall be composed of such directorates as the Public Services Management may direct and may include the following— (a) administration and Finance; (b) public Safety and Legal Section; (c) policy and Research; (d) supervision and Monitoring; (e) training; (f) national Fire and Rescue Training Centre. (2) The administration of the Services shall be divided into Regions and Districts and each region and or district shall be headed by a senior officer and shall be designated as Regional Fire Officers and District Fire Officers respectively. Part III – The Public Service

provisions.title: The roles of the Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: (1) The core function of the Services is to ensure the United Republic of Tanzania is safe against all fire damages and other calamites. This is done by formulating policies that suits the locality but of international standards. (2) The Fire and Rescue Services is responsible for the implementation of policies, strategic programs, plans and schedules regarding fire and rescue impartially. (3) The Services as an overseer of fire and safety matters shall possess skilled officers for implementation strategies and plans. (4) The Fire and Rescue Services shall be responsible for monitoring, supervising, evaluating and fire investigating. The Services shall take legal action against defaulters.

provisions.title: The Fireman ’s duty to the government
provisions.content: (1) Every Fireman owes loyalty, dignity and integrity to the government and in particular to The Fire and Rescue Services Department in which he serves. (2) Every fireman shall serve his government in accordance with the laid down principles in this scheme stipulating:— (a) the duty, rights of fireman to discharge public function reasonably and according to the law; (b) the accountability of the fireman to their leaders and to their supervisors; (c) the duty to comply with the law to uphold the administration of justice; and (d) ethical standard governing particular professions.

provisions.title: Terms and conditions of services
provisions.content: (1) The terms and conditions of the Fire and Rescue Services shall be:— (a) permanent and Pensionable; (b) agreement or Contract; (c) temporary. (2) The terms and conditions of the Fire and Rescue Services provided for under sub-clause (1) of this clause may be modified by any special terms contained in the agreement, contract or letter of appointment of the individual fireman.

provisions.title: Appointment in the Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: Appointments in the Fire and Rescue Services shall be governed by: (a) the constitution of The United Republic of Tanzania 1977; (b) the Public Service Act No. 8 of 2002 ; (c) the Public Service Regulations of 2003; (d) the Fire and Rescue administrative instruction and standing orders; (e) the Public Service code of ethics and conduct; (f) the Public Service professional codes of ethics and conduct; (g) the Fire and Rescue Services Codes of Ethics & Conduct; (h) the Fire and Rescue Services acts.

provisions.title: Qualification for appoint­ments and promotions
provisions.content: No person shall be appointed in the Fire and Rescue Services unless he meets the following necessary requirements:— (a) academic qualification determined by a certain level of education required in the post concerned as indicated in the relevant scheme of Services; (b) professional competence determined by the skill level of the profession; (c) age qualification determined by the date of birth shown either in a birth certificate or any other document relevant and legally acceptable; (d) good character and integrity by his appearance, interaction with other people, the manner he conducts himself, the manner he expresses himself and his track record; (e) experience determined by length of time he has served in the same post or equivalent post; (f) citizenship determined by birth certificate or registration certificate; and (g) any other qualifications as may be prescribed in that post.

provisions.title: Approved establish­ment
provisions.content: (1) No appointments may be made in the Fire and Rescue Services whether temporarily or otherwise for which no provision has been made in the estimates. (2) No increase in the number of posts may be made above the authorized establishment without prior approval of Permanent Secretary (Establishments).

provisions.title: Notifi­cation of vacancies
provisions.content: Notification of vacancies shall specify the necessary requirements for the vacant posts and shall follow the procedures provided in Regulation 7, 8, 9, 10 and 15 of the Public Services Regulations and Administration directives, instructions or orders.

provisions.title: Person not to be appointed in the Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: (1) Any person who has convicted of a criminal offence involving moral turpitude or who has been dismissed from the Public Services Previously or any Services in the United Republic of Tanzania shall not be appointed to the Fire and Rescue Services without prior sanction of the Chief Secretary. Offences involving moral sentiment turpitude shall include:— (a) an act or behavior that gravely violet the moral sentiment or accepted moral standard of the community; (b) be in immorally culpable quality held to be inherent in some criminal offences as distinguished from others. (2) Any person who has been removed or retired in public interest or retired by retrenchment or redundancy from the public Services shall not be appointed in the Fire and Rescue Services without prior sanction of the Chief Secretary. (3) Any person who is of unsound mind shall not be appointed in the Fire and Rescue Services .

provisions.title: The appointment authorities responsibilities
provisions.content: (1) The ultimate responsibility for appointment of the Fire and Rescue Services personnel in the United Republic shall rest upon the appointing authorities. (2) The appointing authorities in the Fire and Rescue Services shall abide by the principles of fairness, meritocracy and impartially when exercising their powers in respect of matters of recruitments, appointments, promotions, discipline and training of the Fire and Rescue Services personnel.

provisions.title: Appoin­tment principles
provisions.content: (1) Appointments in the Fire and Rescue Services shall be governed by the overriding principles of selection based on merit, the well informal choice individuals who through their abilities experience and qualities match the need of the vacant post concerned. (2) The principles of selection for appointments of Fire and Rescue personnel shall be as provided for in the scheme , Public Service Recruitment code of practice and Fire and Rescue Services Regulation. (3) Appointment in the Fire and Rescue Services shall be free from religious beliefs or political partiality and shall be based on:— (a) merit, fairness and open competition; (b) the prescribed qualifications; (c) principles of equal opportunity for all including gender equality; (d) nature of the post, the size and weight of responsibility and duties.

provisions.title: Complaints regarding appoint­ments or recruit ­ment
provisions.content: (1) Where a candidate is dissatisfied with the way appointment procedures are conducted to any post which he is interested arid which he was a candidate, he shall Complain to the authority concerned and if not satisfied with the decision of the appointment authority, he shall appeal to the Public Services Commission and thereafter to the Chief Secretary. (2) The recruitment process shall be fully documented and the document shall be kept for record purposes for a period of not less than twelve months from the date when such recruitment process ended.

provisions.title: Promotions
provisions.content: Promotion in the Fire and Rescue Services shall be dealt within the same manner as new appointments and shall follow the procedure laid down in Regulations 7, 8, 9, 10 and 15 of the Public Service Regulations 2003.

provisions.title: Termi­nation of appoint­ment
provisions.content: (1) Termination of appointments of the Fire and Rescue Services shall include:— (a) removal in public interest; (b) termination by age of retirement; (c) termination upon contesting constitutional leadership or elective political posts; (d) termination on disciplinary ground; (e) termination on medical ground; (f) termination on death; and (g) termination on abolition of office or reorganization of department. (2) Termination of appointment of Fire and Rescue personnel upon contesting a constitutional leadership or electing political post shall take into consideration the provision of regulation 34 of the Public Service Regulations 2003. (3) Recommendation for retrieval shall be made by the Commissioner himself for all gazetted officers and inspectors in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations, and shall only be made where he is of the opinion that grounds for such suggestion do not warrant disciplinary action.

provisions.title: Fire and Rescue Services Personnel accountability
provisions.content: (1) The accountability in the Fire and Rescue Services shall be hierarchical and be based on the hierarchy of authority and responsibility as explained in the scheme . (2) In the highest rank there shall be Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services who is the Chief Commanding officer according to section 5(1)(b)(ii) of the Act . (3) Below the Commissioner , there shall be the head of directorates and below directorates there shall be tlie head of units comprising of Senior, middle and junior levels of Fire and Rescue Services as elaborated in the scheme . (4) Without prejudice to the provision of sub-clause (2) and (3) of this clause, all firemen and Fire Officers shall be accountable to the Commissioner for Fire and Rescue Services . (5) The Commissioner shall be accountable for the deeds for all firemen and or Fire Officers in his Service. (6) The Deputy Commissioner is accountable for the action of all Firemen and, or Fire Officers below him, and every officer in-charge of any specific unit or area shall be accountable for the efficient performance in his respective area of responsibility as elaborated in the scheme . (7) Notwithstanding the provision of sub clause (1), (2), (3), (4), (5) and (6) of this clause every Fireman and or Fire Officer shall be accountable for his own performance and action provided that no interference, undue influence or pressure is exerted upon him by his senior or any Public leader.

provisions.title: Relation between Fireman or Fire Officer and his supervisor
provisions.content: (1) Every Fireman or Fire Officer shall be loyal to the President of the United Republic of Tanzania and shall perform his duties with regard to the requirement of the constitution , laws, regulations, orders, directives and instructions. (2) In performing duties every fireman or Fire Officer shall comply with lawful instructions of his Supervisor, shall strive to serve loyalty and shall respect and co-operate with his fellows at Fire stations, place of work and or at firefighting scare and elsewhere within the Public Services in order to achieve the objective of the Services and Public Service. (3) Every fireman and, or Fire Officer is entitled to recognition and respect of his dignity regardless of hierarchy, discipline or Services in the Fire and Rescue Services . (4) Subject to the laid down procedures a Fireman and, or Fire Officer shall have the right to claim and inquire on any issue pertaining his Services and his senior shall grant the right claimed or avail him with the appropriate or necessary information inquired.

provisions.title: Relatio­nship between a political leader and Fireman and or Fire Officer
provisions.content: There shall not be any political interference with the Fireman and, or Fire Officer while performing his executive duties and functions as elaborated in the scheme and where necessary it shall be through the appropriate supervisors.

provisions.title: Communi­cation with the Fire and Rescue Services
provisions.content: The method of communication within the Fire and Rescue Services shall be as provided for in Public Service Regulations, orders and directives.

provisions.title: The Fire and Rescue Services working document
provisions.content: The necessary Fire and Rescue Services working documents shall include:— (a) the constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania 1977; (b) the Public Service Act of 2002; (c) employment and Labour Relations Act No. 6 of 2004 ; (d) the Public Services Regulations, 2003; (e) the Public Services code of ethics and conduct; (f) Standing Order of the Public Services ; (g) Public Services Recruitment code of practice; (h) the Public Services Disciplinary code of practice; (i) the Public Services management and employment Policy; (j) any other laws, documents and regulations relevant for reference in relation to specific matters; and (k) the National Security Act of 1970.

provisions.title: Perfor­mance Appraisal System
provisions.content: There shall be operated Open Performance Appraisal System to every Fireman and Fire Officer within the Fire and Rescue Services in accordance with procedures provided for in the Public Services Regulations.

provisions.title: Perfo­rmance contracts
provisions.content: Every Fireman and or Fire Officer shall sign a performance contract with his Officer in-charge, head of section, Regional, District Fire Officer and or his Supervisor in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Services Regulations, provided that the Commissioner shall sign and be appraised by the Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Fire and Rescue Services .

provisions.title: Training and staff develop­ment
provisions.content: The Commissioner and Employers shall be responsible for training and developing of their Firemen and Fire Officers in their areas of jurisdiction for the purpose of developing individual skills for the performance of their present duties and future jobs.

provisions.title: Transfer
provisions.content: (1) (a) transfer within the service should be facilitated by the Permanent Secretary responsible for Fire and Rescue Services . (b) ministerial or departmental transfers should be facilitated by Permanent Secretary Public Services Management for the purpose of improving efficiency of the Fire and Rescue Services . (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub clause (1) of this clause, a Fire and Rescue Services personnel shall not be transferred from the Fire and Rescue Services station to another with different terms and condition of Services without his consent. (3) Where the Fire and Rescue Services personnel is transferred he shall be entitled to free transport for himself, spouse and four children or dependants as well as his personal belongings.

provisions.title: Medical services
provisions.content: (1) Every Fire and Rescue Services personnel serving in whatever term and rank shall be entitled to free medical attention for himself, spouse and up to four children or dependants. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub clause (1) of this clause every Fireman and or Fire Officer shall take all reasonable precautions in accordance with modern health practice for the proper protection of his health.

provisions.title: Commis­sioner's duty to protect the health of firemen and or fire officers
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of the Commissioner , or Officer in-charge to take all reasonable precautions to ensure occupational safety standards in order to avoid unnecessary causes of health hazards in his jurisdiction. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub-clause (1) of this clause every Fireman and or Fire Officer shall have a duty to protect him from any hazards.

provisions.title: Leave
provisions.content: (1) Every Fireman and, or Fire Officer shall be entitled to annual leave which shall be granted by the employer once a year in accordance with the Public Services Regulations, Orders and Directives. (2) Where the fireman or Fire Officer is denied his annual leave rights by the employer due to exigencies of Services, he shall be entitled to a one month salary payment in lieu there of.

provisions.title: Compen­sation for injuries or death
provisions.content: Where a Fire and Rescue Services personnel sustain injuries or dies in the cause of his employment, he shall be entitled to compensation which shall be paid in accordance with the procedures provided in Public Service Regulations of 2003, Orders and Directives.

provisions.title: Grievances handling mechanism
provisions.content: It shall be the role of the Commissioner or Officer in-charge of the Fire and Rescue Services to establish a grievances handling mechanism and shall receive, solve or seek solution for grievances of Fire and Rescue Services personnel under his respective area of jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Duty to keep record
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of the employer and employee to keep employment records for the purposes of reference for calculation of the employee ’s terminal benefits. (2) Employment records kept by both employer and employee shall be regarded as original copies duly signed by authorized person. (3) Where there is no possibility to get hold of employment records from employers, records in the custody of the employee shall be used for any purpose provided that such records are in the original form. Part IV – Discipline General

provisions.title: Conduct of Fire and Rescue Services personnel
provisions.content: (1) Every Fireman or Fire Officer of the Fire and Rescue Services shall conduct himself with integrity, impartiality and honesty. He shall give honest and impartial advice to the President or, as the case may be, to any Minister , to the Commissioner or to the Head of Directorates or Department or to his Senior Officer without fear or favour and Senior Officer without fear or favour shall make all information relevant for decision available to them. (2) A Fireman or Fire officer shall not deceive or knowingly mislead the President, Minister , National Assembly, Commissioner or his Senior Officer or any fireman in the Fire and Rescue Services and the public in general. (3) Every fireman or fire officer shall not conduct himself in a way which:— (a) is illegal; (b) is improper; (c) is unethical; (d) is in breach of constitutional convention of professional code of ethics and conduct; and (e) is inconsistent with the Public Service code of Ethics and Conduct, Public Service Leadership Code of Ethics and Conduct, Orders, Directives and commands. (4) Firemen integrity and behavior A fireman and or fire officer shall conduct himself in accordance with the provision of; (a) disciplinary Code of Conduct; (b) the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct; (c) the Public Leadership Code of Ethics and Conduct; (d) various professional codes of ethics and conduct; and (e) The Public Service Regulations, administrative instructions and orders. (5) Confidentiality Every fireman shall serve with high standards of wisdom and integrity and shall behave as a servant with public trust on safety command and not as a master and shall show normal courtesy; (6) (a) any fireman who is aware of any breach or who has been required by any other fireman whether Senior or junior to act in a way which raises a fundamental issue of conscience of that fireman or fire officer, may report that breach to his supervisor or his Commissioner or to the Permanent Secretary responsible for Fire and Rescue Services as the case may be. (b) any firemen who contravene the provisions of this clause shall commit a disciplinary offence and shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Fire and Rescue Service Regulations. (c) Political involvement of fireman a fireman has the right of being a member of any political party and can vote both for his political party and in general elections except where he is, by instruction prohibited by any law, Regulations or administrative Directives or Orders. (d) Political neutrality and impartiality of a fireman a fireman has to be neutral and impartial while delivering Services to the public. He shall serve without any bias due to his political affiliation and shall not pass information or documents availed through his position in the Services to any political party. (e) subject to the provision of this part of this scheme any offence against or any other misconduct by any fire officer shall be dealt with in accordance with this scheme . (7) (i) The powers of discipline in respect of Fire officers shall be exercised by the Fire and Rescue Services Commissioner . (ii) The offences specified in the first schedule part A and B to the Public Service Regulations and provisions of the professional code of conduct for the Fire and Rescue Services appended to this Scheme shall form part of this Scheme and violation thereof may warrant disciplinary proceedings. 8. Disciplinary authority in case of fire and rescue offences Any fire officer who:— (i) persuades or endeavor to persuade procures attempts to procure or assist, any fire officer to desert, or being cognizant of any such desertion or intended desertion does not without delay give information thereof to his senior officer; (ii) uses violence against a fire officer; (iii) is disrespectful in word, act or demeanor to his senior officer; (iv) refuses to obey or willfully disobeys or neglects to obey any lawful command; (v) renders himself unfit for duty by intoxication or the effect of drugs; (vi) drinks intoxicating liquors when on duty; (vii) is drunk on duty or in any services lines or in any place while wearing uniforms; (viii) absents himself without leave; (ix) is asleep while on duty; (x) being under arrest or confinement, leaves or escapes froth his arrest or confinement before he is set at liberty; (xi) is guilty of cowardice; (xii) fires any firearm without orders or just cause; (xiii) without cause fails to appear at any parade appointed by his senior officer; (xiv) pawns sells, loses by neglects, make away with willfully damages or fails to report any damage to any arm, ammunition, accoutrement, uniform or other article supplied to him or any property committed to his charge; (xv) is in unlawful possession of any public property or any property of any other members of the services ; (xvi) solicits or accepts any free, bribe or gratuity; (xvii) is idle or negligent on the performance of his duty; (xviii) is slovenly inattentive, uncivil or quarrel some; (xix) parades for duty dirty or untidy in his person, arms, clothing or equipment; (xx) without due authority discloses or conveys any information concerning any services matter; (xxi) makes or sign any false statement in any official record or document; (xxii) makes or joins in making any anonymous complaints; (xxiii) malingers or feigns or willfully causes any diseased or infirmity; (xxiv) without proper authority extracts from any person carriage, portage or provisions; (xxv) makes any false statement on becoming a fire officer; (xxvi) refuses or neglects or sends any report of return which accusation to be false; (xxvii) engages without authority in any employment other than his duty as fire officer; (xxviii) makes a false accusation against any fire officer knowing such accusation to be false; (xxix) willfully disobeys any regulation order or the services ; (xxx) neglects or refuses to assist in apprehension of any member of the services charged with any offence when lawfully ordered so to do; (xxxi) uses unwarrantable personal violence to any person whilst on duty; (xxxii) leave his post before he is properly and regularly delivered except in sudden rush from danger or to an emergency; (xxxiii) is guilty of any act, conduct, disorders, neglect to the prejudice of good order or disciplinary of the services , or security of the services , in violation of duty in his office, or any other misconduct as a member of the service not here in before specified, shall be deemed to have committed offence against discipline and shall suffer punishment according to the offence in accordance with the provision of these regulations; (xxxiv) commits or causes or permits any acts to be committed or connives at any act which is prejudicial to the administration, discipline and efficiency of the fire and rescue services or to the authority or position of a member of the fire and rescue services . (xxxv) make use of his position in the fire and rescue services to promote or to prejudice the interest of any political party. (xxxvi) acts or omission which tends to bring the fire and rescue services into disrepute; (xxxvii) using without consent of the prescribed authority any property or facilities for the purpose of the fire and rescue services for some purposes not commended with the fire and rescue services . Part V – Miscellaneous A. Allowances and retirement benefits

provisions.title: Allowances payable to Firemen and or Fire Officers
provisions.content: (1) All Firemen and Fire Officers shall be entitled to the following allowances:— (a) ration allowances; (b) risk allowances; and (c) head sitting allowances. (2) The following allowances shall be payable to eligible officers as may be prescribed:— (a) extra duty allowances; (b) honorarium; (c) outfit allowance; (d) disturbance allowance; (e) acting allowance; (f) subsistence allowance; and (g) housing maintenance allowance.

provisions.title: Other allowances for Firemen or Fire Officers
provisions.content: Without prejudice to the foregoing provision Firemen and or Fire Officers shall be entitled to any other allowances as stipulated from time to time through circulars, orders, directives by the Public Service Management.

provisions.title: Age of retirement
provisions.content: (1) Age of retirement for a Fireman and Fire Officer shall be as provided for in the Public Service Retirement Benefit Act applicable for the time being. (2) Where there is ambiguity on the date of birth of a fireman or Fire Officer and where there is argument between the employer and a Fireman or Fire Officer as to accurate date of birth, the date on record disclosed by the Fireman and or Fire Officer during his first appointment shall be recognized as the accurate date of birth of such Fireman or Fire Officer . (3) Where only the year and not the actual date of birth of a Fireman or Fire Officer have been recorded, his date of birth shall be deemed to be the first day of July of that year. B. Pension and gratuities

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: The provision of this part shall apply to non pensionable fire officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector . You can not be a pension officer before working or be in Services for 10 years then you ought to apply to be pensionable officer.

provisions.title: When gratuity may be granted
provisions.content: A Fireman or Fire Officer to whom this clause applies shall on discharge (other than discharge on dismissal from the Services for misconduct) after a continuous good Service extending over a period of not less than three years be eligible to receive a gratuity calculated at the rate of rank of the fireman in which he was serving at the date of his discharge for every completed year of Service.

provisions.title: Where fireman or fire officers dies while on contract
provisions.content: Any gratuity which would have been due and payable to a Fireman or Fire Officer to whom this part applies dies before he had taken his discharge under the provisions of this scheme shall in the event of his re­engaging for further term and dying in the Services before the completion of such term, be deemed to have belonged to him for the purpose of the administration of his estates.

provisions.title: Where death occurs before receipt of gratuity
provisions.content: Where Fireman or Fire Officer to whom this part applies having been discharged, dies before receipt by him any gratuity for which he has become eligible under this scheme , such gratuity shall be paid to the person authorized to administer his estate. The person authorized must be paid three months salary to make (him/her to live) when in follow-up of the gratuity.

provisions.title: Recovery of debt from gratuity
provisions.content: Notwithstanding any of the provisions of this scheme , where at the time of the discharge of a Fireman or Fire Officer to whom this part applies from the Fire and Rescue Services , such Fireman or Fire Officer has failed to pay any outstanding bills owed by him to any Fire and Rescue Services Institutions any gratuity payable may at any time be withheld until satisfactory arrangements have been made for the settlement of such accounts or bills as aforesaid either by the Fireman or Fire Officer or by deductions from such gratuity.

provisions.title: Gratuity not assignable
provisions.content: Except as otherwise provided in this scheme a gratuity granted under the provision shall not be assigned or transferable except for the purposes of satisfying:— (i) Debt due to the government; or (ii) An order of any competent count for the periodical payment of sums of money towards the maintenance of the wife or former wife or child of the Fire Officer to whom the gratuity has been granted. Part VI – Miscellaneous

provisions.title: Prolongation of Services in case of War etc.
provisions.content: Notwithstanding any other provision of this scheme any Fireman or Fire Officer whose period of Services expires during a state of War, insurrection or hostilities, may be retained as the Commissioner may direct. Schedule (Made under Regulation 65 (20)) Code of ethics and conduct for Fire and Rescue Services The Services is under command and control of the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services, whose powers are prescribed in the Public Services Act regulations and the Services scheme provided that the Commissioner may authorize to officer such powers in accordance to reg.49(5) of the Public Services Regulation 2003. In order for the Fire and Rescue Services to be efficient and respected, Fire men and Fire Officers must adhere to the professional code of ethics and conduct hereunder prescribed:— 1. Discipline and integrity: (i) obey the lawful order of his superior. (ii) conduct in a proper manner at all times when on and off duty. (iii) shall not absent him from fire stations without permission of his/her superior. (iv) shall not be intoxicated in uniform or on duty. (v) shall not use violence’s against his/her fellow fire officer. (vi) shall not be disrespectful in word, act, or demeanor to his senior officer. (vii) being under arrest or confinement, shall not leave or escape from his arrest or confinement before he is set free. (viii) shall not solicit or accept any bribery or gratuity. (ix) shall not be idle or negligent while performing his/her duty. (x) shall be punctual with respect to hours at all events and calamities. (xi) shall not make or sign any false statement in any official record or document. (xii) shall not fire any firearm without orders or just cause or use it recklessly. (xiii) all firemen and fire officers on or off duty will attend a roll call parade dressed in attire each morning and inspection shall be conducted by any senior officer. (xiv) the mode of greeting will be in salute junior fire officers will salute senior fire officers are in uniform or not. (xv) the Fireman must serve the public with dignity, humanity, integrity, and using wisdom. 2. Transparence and accountability: (i) every Fireman and Fire Officer in delivering Services shall be accountable for actions and inactions through normal tiers. (ii) all Regional Fire Officers and Senior Fire Officer in charge of Fire Stations are accountable to the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue in their jurisdictions. (iii) all Firemen and Fire Officer are accountable to the officer in charge of the Fire stations for all duties assigned to them. (iv) all Fire Stations must have duty roasters maintained and checked by officer in charge of the Fire stations. (v) during emergency all information shall be availed transparently to all members of the Services for easy mobilization to suppress the calamity. 3. Respect to human rights and courtesy: (i) every fireman or fire officer shall not use violence against the member of the Services. He or she shall avoid all type of conduct, which may infringe the right of his fellow employee. (ii) every Fireman or Fire officer shall be courteous to Senior Officers and fellow fireman or fire officer as well as to all clients. (iii) every fireman and Fire Officer shall respect fellow firemen rights as well as their rights to privacy. (iv) every Fireman or fire Officer shall not discriminate or harass a member of the Services or fellow employee on the ground of sex, tribe or ethnicity. (v) every Fireman or Fire Officer shall have the right to claim and inquire to any issue pertaining his Services through administrative channels based on hierarchical order. (vi) every fireman and Fire Officers is entitled to recognition and respect of his/her dignity regardless of the hierarchy in Fire and Rescue Services. (vii) when it becomes necessary to reprove a member of the staff, this must not be done in front of junior members. 4. Team work and cooperation: (i) all firemen and fire officers shall work collectively to suppress any disaster or eventuality. (ii) Firemen and fire Officers shall not in any way prejudice efforts by his/her fellow staff during the operations. (iii) staff welfare Services centers shall be established and sports encouraged promoting atmosphere of cooperation and teamwork. (iv) fire and Rescue Senior Officers shall be responsible for the welfare of their subordinate staff and their families under their jurisdiction. 5. Accoutrement, dressing, and respectable attire: (i) all Firemen and Fire Officers shall wear uniforms in attire and in accordance to scheme of Services while on duty. (ii) all firemen and fire officers shall wear uniforms while on transfers, escorts and while attending Kit Inspection parades. (iii) Fireman in uniform shall not smoke, drink alcohol and shall not conduct himself in a manner that will bring the public into the disrepute. (iv) irrespective of their conditions, items will be submitted to the officer in charge of the station immediately after the termination of Services. Such items shall include uniforms, shoes, caps badge, belt, whistle yanard, buttons, shoulder titles, socks, bandages, chevrons and other accoutrement related to his or her duties. 6. Physical drills: Every Fireman will be obliged to attend weekly physical drills and monthly drills to senior Fire officers to make them physically fit for operation. They must be in appropriate uniforms and accoutrement. 7. Use of information, communication and records: (i) All official correspondence intended for the headquarters office will be addressed to the Commissioner of fire and Rescue Services. (ii) Firemen and Fire Officers shall not disclose confidential or official information which has been communicated to or has been availed while discharging official duties without due permission. Fire Officers shall continue to maintain secrecy and confidentiality of official information even after one has left the Services. (iii) Demi-official correspondence may be sent to the Commissioner by all officers through an independent command. Copies of such correspondence should be filed, but a demi-official reply from the Commissioner should not be treated as official. Official confirmation should be obtained before action. (iv) Senior Officers shall give commands, orders and instructions, which are clear and undistorted. (v) The officer in charge of station shall keep registers, documents, records, leave roasters and other documents required under Public Services Regulations. 8. Reporting of events: (i) The Commissioner will be informed immediately of all cases of calamities and of all cases of members of staff sustained injuries as a result of an accident encountered during operations. (ii) All Regional Fire Officers and in charge of stations shall report monthly all matters of fire and safety to the Commissioner of Fire and Rescue Services. 9. Conclusion: The code of Fire and Rescue professional ethics and conduct has been issued in pursuant to Public Services Act, geared to safeguard norms and standards embodied in Fire and Rescue Services. The code of conduct will operate in tandem with other instruments such as— (i) Public Services Act. (ii) Public Services Regulations. (iii) Public Services Scheme. (iv) Code of Ethics and conduct for Public Services. (v) Fire and Rescue Scheme of Services.
